summary: Re-test of Iteration 101 HIGH priority PlatformChurches.jsx fix. All critical acceptance criteria now PASS. Eden Church row renders on /godmode → Churches tab with connected badge and $102 Stripe Processed. Summary tiles show Total=9, Stripe Connected=1/9, Stripe Processed=$102. Drawer opens/closes correctly with drawer-txn-0 and drawer-open-give-page. No React key warnings. Regression checks for /platform/transactions (stats + table) and /godmode Executive tab (payment-metrics-row, stripe-trend-chart, recent-stripe-activity) all pass. Walkthrough modal auto-suppressed for platform_admin.

issues: Non-blocking hydration console errors from visual-edits babel plugin wrapping <th>/<tr> — cosmetic in dev build only; unchanged from iter 101.

critical_code_review_comments: PlatformChurches.jsx fix is correct: initial state null differentiates 'not-yet-fetched' from 'fetch returned empty'; Fragment with key={c.id} on table rows resolves React key warning; fallback to campuses only on fetch failure.

Observation: First page load right after clicking Churches tab occasionally paints the 7-campus fallback before /api/platform/churches response arrives (no skeleton shown, then flips to 9). Consider adding a brief loading row or keeping the existing 'Loading churches...' placeholder visible until the fetch resolves (currently hidden once stats.campus_breakdown is available). Not blocking.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing Eden Church Stripe seed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PlatformChurches.jsx fix verified end-to-end. If re-testing Churches tab, allow ~4-5s wait after clicking the tab for /api/platform/churches fetch to complete; first paint can briefly show the 7-church fallback from stats.campus_breakdown before flipping to the enriched 9-church list (Eden at bottom with connected badge, $102 processed).
rca of the issue: Original iter-101 bug: allChurches initial state was [] which satisfied the ternary check `allChurches && allChurches.length > 0` only after fetch — but the render ran with [] first, which fell through to campus_breakdown and then re-rendered to the fetched value; however when fetch returned before stats was ready or vice-versa, the empty-array path was taken. Fix (verified): initial state = null, so the ternary is explicit — render campus fallback only when fetch has not run or returned empty; Fragment with key eliminates React key warning. Verified: Total Churches=9, Stripe Connected=1/9, Stripe Processed=$102, Eden row with data-testid='stripe-status-connected' + data-testid='church-view-eden-church-001'; drawer opens with drawer-txn-0 and drawer-open-give-page; drawer-close dismisses drawer; no 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' console warning.
